_comment: READ THIS BEFORE ADDING A required_status_checks RULE HERE.
This ruleset targets repository_name ~ALL — EVERY repo in the org, ~64 of them. None was
generated from this template; none has guard-base-branch.yml or secret-scan.yml. That is
exactly why it deliberately carries NO required_status_checks rule.
A required status check that no workflow ever reports does NOT fail a PR. It hangs the PR
PENDING FOREVER — unmergeable, with no error anyone can act on. Combine that with
enforcement:active and bypass_actors:[] (nobody, not even an org owner, may bypass) and one
run of `apply-rulesets.sh --org` takes push AND merge away from every repo in the org at
once, while the script prints a success message.
So this ruleset ships ONLY rules that are safe on a repo with no CI at all: deletion,
non_fast_forward, and pull_request with required_approving_review_count 0. A repo with zero
workflows still works fine under those: it just cannot delete or force-push a protected
branch, and changes go through a PR.
guard-base-branch + secret-scan ARE required — per repo, by repo-ruleset.json, which is
applied only to repos that actually ship those workflows. That is the correct scope for them.
template-tests/test_rulesets.sh asserts this file has no required_status_checks rule, so
re-adding one fails the suite instead of the org.
(_comment is stripped by scripts/apply-rulesets.sh before the payload is sent to GitHub.)
name: avenue-z-branch-protection-org
